reviews.review_title: EDITed AND ADDED STAR!! Ty devs
reviews.review_description: 4 out of 5 stars til update and was fast to get a solution for my seated issue problem and it was fast too🩷So adding star for even considering it and and adding so quickly!! Ty I absolutely enjoyed this update seated mode and even though stil tough to get there I made it the end of demo w what’s been done already. the only thing I would add is a sensitivity setting for how hard the bouncer has to hit ground and  and also lowering the inventory to choose sticks. I still don’t think I’d get too far with no changes further without these two things but congrats on this gem keep it up and will b watching to see if anything else changes BUT it’s a no brainer guys buy it Ty for creating such a fun  game and even careing about what your players want that alone is 5 stars right there for me everytime. Get this game u won’t regret it sitting or not🥰🥰

As someone who tests a large number of VR games every day in SideQuest QA, it is rare that a project genuinely excites me. This platformer managed to do exactly that. The idea with the different rods, such as the magnet rod and others, is innovative and truly unique, adding a lot of depth to the gameplay. Everything feels crafted with real attention to detail, which shows in every part of the experience. The game is very polished. After about forty minutes of playtime I did not encounter a single bug. The visuals are charming and beautifully designed, with high resolution and well baked lighting. I assume it was created in Unreal, though I might be mistaken. Once the full version releases, this will be an easy purchase recommendation 😊

reviews.review_title: 🌟 A Whimsical Journey Full of Surprises
reviews.review_description:
Endless Wonder is a delightful surprise and a very promising VR demo. The title perfectly matches the experience, as you move from one “wonder” to another — with a vibe reminiscent of Alice in Wonderland mixed with Super Mario 64 exploration.

The game is elegantly crafted, fun to navigate, and genuinely enjoyable to explore. Multiple game mechanics and tools keep progression engaging, and the overall execution feels polished and confident. While it’s currently a demo, it already has a very solid foundation and all the ingredients to become a future hit.

reviews.review_title: So where the hell did this come from?
reviews.review_description: I downloaded this demo thinking it would just be a little time waster but ended up having a blast and I didn't want to put it down. The magnetic moving is so different than anything I've ever tried. I think you guys might be on to something special and even if this game doesn't blow up then I think your talent will come through eventually because this has some pretty fun original ideas

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your feedback—we truly appreciate it!

Unfortunately, you’re right—the store algorithms seem to completely ignore our game, so it’s unlikely to gain much visibility. Because of this, we’ve decided not to proceed with developing a sequel, as we can’t afford to spend years on development only to end up effectively hidden in the store.
